operators.name: Shared Mem In / Out
operators.translation: This lets two programs pass notes through a shared mailbox. If one side writes before the other is ready, the note is missing or old.

operators.name: Ambient Light
operators.translation: This is like turning on a room light that brightens everything evenly, without caring where objects are.

operators.name: Blend
operators.translation: This mixes two pictures together. Which one is on top matters, like stacking papers.

operators.name: Bone
operators.translation: This is a bendy joint in a puppet. It remembers how it was posed and changes over time.

operators.name: Camera
operators.translation: This is the eye that looks at the scene. What you see depends on where the eye is and what it points at.

operators.name: Camera Blend
operators.translation: This slowly moves between two eyes, like fading between camera shots.

operators.name: Environment Light
operators.translation: This wraps the whole world in light from a big picture, like standing inside a glowing bubble.

operators.name: FBX
operators.translation: This brings in a boxed-up 3D thing from outside. It has to be unpacked first before it works.

operators.name: Geometry
operators.translation: This is a container that turns raw shapes into something the renderer can see.

operators.name: Handle
operators.translation: This is the grabby gizmo you drag in the viewer. It helps you move things but doesn’t change the math by itself.

operators.name: Light
operators.translation: This is a flashlight in the scene. Where it is and what it points at changes how things look.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This is a labeled hook in the network. It holds a place so you can connect things neatly.

operators.name: USD
operators.translation: This brings in a big organized world description. It sets rules first, then everything follows them.

operators.name: Shared Mem In / Out
operators.translation: This is a shared mailbox between programs. If one writes too early or too late, the message can be missing or old.

operators.name: Ambient Light
operators.translation: This is like turning on a room light that makes everything brighter the same way.

operators.name: Blend
operators.translation: This mixes two pictures together. Which one goes first changes the result.

operators.name: Bone
operators.translation: This is a bendy joint for a puppet. It remembers how it moved before and keeps changing over time.

operators.name: Camera
operators.translation: This is the eye that looks at the scene. What you see depends on where the eye is.

operators.name: Camera Blend
operators.translation: This fades between two eyes, like slowly switching camera shots.

operators.name: Environment Light
operators.translation: This lights the whole scene using a big surrounding picture, like standing inside a glowing sky.

operators.name: FBX
operators.translation: This brings in a packed 3D object from outside that has to be set up before it works.

operators.name: Geometry
operators.translation: This is a box that holds shapes and makes them ready to be drawn.

operators.name: Handle
operators.translation: This is the grab tool you drag in the viewer. It helps you move things but doesn’t do math by itself.

operators.name: Light
operators.translation: This is a flashlight in the scene. Moving it changes how objects look.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This is a named plug you use to keep networks organized.

operators.name: USD
operators.translation: This loads a big structured world and sets the rules before anything else happens.

operators.name: Actor
operators.translation: This is a character in a physics world. It remembers where it was last frame and keeps moving based on that.

operators.name: Bullet Solver
operators.translation: This is the referee that steps time forward for physics. If the timing or order changes, the whole game changes.

operators.name: Constraint
operators.translation: This is a rule that tells two things how they are allowed to move together, like tying them with a rope.

operators.name: Force
operators.translation: This is a push or pull that gets applied every moment. When it happens matters just as much as how strong it is.

operators.name: Add
operators.translation: This adds pictures together like stacking light. The order and values change how bright it gets.

operators.name: Analyze
operators.translation: This looks at a picture and measures things like brightness or color amounts.

operators.name: Anti-Alias
operators.translation: This smooths jagged edges by blending nearby pixels together.

operators.name: Cache
operators.translation: This remembers previous frames so you can look back in time.

operators.name: Cache Select
operators.translation: This picks a stored frame from memory, like choosing a photo from a stack.

operators.name: Chop to TOP
operators.translation: This turns numbers over time into an image.

operators.name: Composite
operators.translation: This combines images using rules like over, add, or multiply.

operators.name: Constant
operators.translation: This makes a flat image that never changes unless you change its settings.

operators.name: C++
operators.translation: This runs custom code to make images. What it does depends entirely on the code.

operators.name: Direct Display Out
operators.translation: This sends the picture straight to a display.

operators.name: DirectX 10
operators.translation: This uses DirectX to draw images using the graphics card.

operators.name: Import Select
operators.translation: This reaches into another place and pulls an image out.

operators.name: Movie File In
operators.translation: This plays a movie file frame by frame over time.

operators.name: NDI In
operators.translation: This receives live video over a network.

operators.name: NCam
operators.translation: This brings in live camera tracking data as images.

operators.name: OCSelect
operators.translation: This pulls an image out of a component.

operators.name: Orbex Select
operators.translation: This selects image data coming from Orbex devices.

operators.name: Point File In
operators.translation: This loads point data from a file and turns it into an image.

operators.name: Render Stream In
operators.translation: This receives rendered images from another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Render Stream Out
operators.translation: This sends rendered images to another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Screen Grab
operators.translation: This takes a picture of what’s on the screen.

operators.name: Shared Mem In
operators.translation: This reads images from a shared memory mailbox.

operators.name: Shared Mem Out
operators.translation: This writes images into a shared memory mailbox.

operators.name: Syphon / Spout In
operators.translation: This receives video from another app using Syphon or Spout.

operators.name: Syphon / Spout Out
operators.translation: This sends video to another app using Syphon or Spout.

operators.name: Touch In
operators.translation: This receives data from another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Touch Out
operators.translation: This sends data to another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Video Device In
operators.translation: This brings live video in from a camera or capture card.

operators.name: Video Device Out
operators.translation: This sends video out to external hardware.

operators.name: Web Render
operators.translation: This shows a web page as a moving image.

operators.name: Add
operators.translation: This adds images together like piling light on top of light. Which image goes first changes the result.

operators.name: Analyze
operators.translation: This looks at an image and counts or measures what’s inside it.

operators.name: Anti-Alias
operators.translation: This softens jagged edges by blending nearby pixels.

operators.name: Cache
operators.translation: This remembers past frames so you can use them later.

operators.name: Cache Select
operators.translation: This picks one stored frame from memory.

operators.name: Chop to TOP
operators.translation: This turns rows of numbers into an image.

operators.name: Composite
operators.translation: This combines images using rules like over, add, or multiply.

operators.name: Constant
operators.translation: This makes a flat image that stays the same until you change it.

operators.name: C++
operators.translation: This makes images using custom code, so its behavior depends on the program inside.

operators.name: Direct Display Out
operators.translation: This sends the image straight to a screen.

operators.name: DirectX 10
operators.translation: This draws images using DirectX on the graphics card.

operators.name: Import Select
operators.translation: This reaches into another place and pulls an image out.

operators.name: Movie File In
operators.translation: This plays a movie file one frame at a time.

operators.name: NDI In
operators.translation: This receives live video over a network.

operators.name: NCam
operators.translation: This turns live camera tracking into image data.

operators.name: OCSelect
operators.translation: This pulls an image out of a component.

operators.name: Orbex Select
operators.translation: This selects image data coming from Orbex systems.

operators.name: Point File In
operators.translation: This loads point data from a file and turns it into an image.

operators.name: Render Stream In
operators.translation: This receives rendered images from another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Render Stream Out
operators.translation: This sends rendered images to another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Screen Grab
operators.translation: This takes a snapshot of what’s on the screen.

operators.name: Shared Mem In
operators.translation: This reads images from a shared memory mailbox.

operators.name: Shared Mem Out
operators.translation: This writes images into a shared memory mailbox.

operators.name: Syphon / Spout In
operators.translation: This receives video from another app using Syphon or Spout.

operators.name: Syphon / Spout Out
operators.translation: This sends video to another app using Syphon or Spout.

operators.name: Touch In
operators.translation: This receives data from another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Touch Out
operators.translation: This sends data to another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Video Device In
operators.translation: This brings live video in from a camera or capture device.

operators.name: Video Device Out
operators.translation: This sends video out to external hardware.

operators.name: Web Render
operators.translation: This turns a web page into a moving image.

operators.name: Channel Mix
operators.translation: This takes the color parts of an image and mixes them in new ways, like swapping or blending paint colors.

operators.name: Chroma Key
operators.translation: This removes a color, like green, to make parts of the picture disappear.

operators.name: Constant (Color)
operators.translation: This makes a solid block of one color that stays the same.

operators.name: Edge
operators.translation: This finds where colors change suddenly, like outlining shapes with a pencil.

operators.name: Emboss
operators.translation: This makes the image look raised or pressed in, like a stamped coin.

operators.name: HSV Adjust
operators.translation: This changes color, brightness, or strength of colors using color knobs.

operators.name: HSV to RGB
operators.translation: This changes one way of describing colors into another way.

operators.name: Luma Blur
operators.translation: This blurs only the light and dark parts of an image.

operators.name: Luma Level
operators.translation: This makes the light and dark parts brighter or darker.

operators.name: Math
operators.translation: This uses math rules to change pixel values.

operators.name: Matte
operators.translation: This uses one image to cut holes or shapes out of another.

operators.name: Monochrome
operators.translation: This turns the picture into one color or shades of gray.

operators.name: RGB Key
operators.translation: This removes pixels based on red, green, and blue values.

operators.name: RGB to HSV
operators.translation: This changes how colors are described, from red-green-blue to color-wheel values.

operators.name: Reorder
operators.translation: This rearranges color channels, like swapping left and right socks.

operators.name: Slope
operators.translation: This remaps brightness using a curve, like bending a ruler.

operators.name: Threshold
operators.translation: This turns pixels fully on or off based on a cutoff point.

operators.name: Tone Map
operators.translation: This squeezes very bright and dark values into a range you can see.

operators.name: Corner Pin
operators.translation: This stretches an image by grabbing its four corners and pinning them somewhere else.

operators.name: Crop
operators.translation: This cuts out part of an image and throws the rest away.

operators.name: Displace
operators.translation: This pushes pixels around using another image, like using wind to blow fabric.

operators.name: Fit
operators.translation: This resizes an image so it fits inside another space.

operators.name: Flip
operators.translation: This turns the image over left-right or up-down.

operators.name: Inside
operators.translation: This shows one image only where another image exists.

operators.name: Lens Distortion
operators.translation: This bends the image like it was seen through a curved camera lens.

operators.name: Lookup
operators.translation: This uses a table image to decide what color each pixel becomes.

operators.name: Mirror
operators.translation: This reflects the image across a line, like a mirror.

operators.name: Normal Map
operators.translation: This turns color information into directions that describe surface bumps.

operators.name: Outside
operators.translation: This shows one image only where another image does not exist.

operators.name: Over
operators.translation: This places one image on top of another.

operators.name: Projection
operators.translation: This throws an image onto a surface as if using a projector.

operators.name: Remap
operators.translation: This changes values from one range into another range.

operators.name: Resolution
operators.translation: This changes how many pixels an image has.

operators.name: Screen
operators.translation: This blends images together in a way that makes them brighter.

operators.name: Tile
operators.translation: This repeats an image over and over to fill space.

operators.name: Transform
operators.translation: This moves, rotates, or scales an image.

operators.name: Under
operators.translation: This puts one image underneath another.

operators.name: Bloom
operators.translation: This makes bright parts of an image glow and spill light into nearby areas.

operators.name: Blur
operators.translation: This softens the image by averaging nearby pixels together.

operators.name: Convolve
operators.translation: This changes the image using a small pattern that slides over every pixel, like rubbing a texture across it.

operators.name: Difference
operators.translation: This compares two images and shows where they are not the same.

operators.name: Circle
operators.translation: This draws a round shape into an image, like stamping a circle onto paper.

operators.name: Cross
operators.translation: This draws a plus-shaped mark, like two rectangles crossing.

operators.name: Function
operators.translation: This makes an image by following a math rule, like drawing a picture from a formula.

operators.name: GLSL
operators.translation: This makes images using small programs that run on the graphics card.

operators.name: GLSL Multi
operators.translation: This runs many small graphics programs together, where the order can change the result.

operators.name: Noise
operators.translation: This fills the image with random-looking patterns, like static or clouds.

operators.name: Ramp
operators.translation: This makes a smooth change from one color or value to another.

operators.name: Rectangle
operators.translation: This draws a box shape into an image.

operators.name: Depth
operators.translation: This shows how far away things are from the camera, turning distance into an image.

operators.name: Render
operators.translation: This is the machine that draws the 3D world into a picture based on cameras, lights, and objects.

operators.name: Render Pass
operators.translation: This draws the scene in separate layers, like painting the background and shadows in different steps.

operators.name: Render Select
operators.translation: This picks a specific result out of a render, like choosing only the shadow picture.

operators.name: Render Simple
operators.translation: This draws the scene in a basic way with fewer options.

operators.name: SSAO
operators.translation: This adds soft shadows where objects are close together, like darkening corners and cracks.

operators.name: Blob Track
operators.translation: This watches moving shapes in an image and remembers where they were last frame so it can keep following them.

operators.name: Feedback
operators.translation: This feeds the last frame back into the next one, like pointing a camera at its own screen.

operators.name: Kinect
operators.translation: This brings in live depth and body data from a Kinect sensor.

operators.name: Leap Motion
operators.translation: This brings in live hand and finger tracking data.

operators.name: Motion
operators.translation: This finds where pixels change over time, like spotting movement between frames.

operators.name: Optical Flow
operators.translation: This measures how pixels slide across the image from frame to frame, like tracking the direction of moving clouds.

operators.name: RealSense
operators.translation: This brings in depth and camera data from an Intel RealSense device.

operators.name: ZED
operators.translation: This brings in stereo camera and depth data from a ZED camera.

operators.name: ZED Select
operators.translation: This chooses a specific image or data stream coming from a ZED camera.

operators.name: MPCDI
operators.translation: This loads a big projection-mapping setup so images line up correctly on curved or multiple screens.

operators.name: OpenColorIO
operators.translation: This changes colors using strict rules so they look the same on different screens.

operators.name: OpenVR
operators.translation: This connects the image system to a VR headset so the picture moves with your head.

operators.name: Pack
operators.translation: This squeezes many images into one bigger image, like packing photos into a collage.

operators.name: Prefilter Map
operators.translation: This prepares lighting textures so shiny surfaces know how to reflect light.

operators.name: Scalable Display
operators.translation: This helps spread one image across many screens so they act like one big display.

operators.name: Substance
operators.translation: This turns material recipes into images you can use for textures.

operators.name: ST2110 In
operators.translation: This receives professional broadcast video over a network.

operators.name: ST2110 Out
operators.translation: This sends professional broadcast video over a network.

operators.name: Switch
operators.translation: This chooses which image to pass through, like flipping a light switch.

operators.name: Texture 3D
operators.translation: This stores images as a 3D block so shaders can look inside them.

operators.name: Time Machine
operators.translation: This records frames over time so you can jump backward and forward.

operators.name: Vioso
operators.translation: This aligns images to real-world projection surfaces using calibration data.

operators.name: Window
operators.translation: This opens a window on the screen to show the image.

operators.name: Ableton Link
operators.translation: This keeps time in sync with other music apps, like everyone clapping to the same beat.

operators.name: Angle
operators.translation: This turns movement or direction into angle numbers.

operators.name: Attribute
operators.translation: This pulls numeric information out of 3D data and turns it into channels.

operators.name: Audio Band EQ
operators.translation: This lets you make some sound ranges louder or quieter, like turning knobs for bass and treble.

operators.name: Audio Binaural
operators.translation: This makes sound feel like it’s coming from around your head.

operators.name: Audio Device In
operators.translation: This brings sound in from a microphone or sound card.

operators.name: Audio Device Out
operators.translation: This sends sound out to speakers or headphones.

operators.name: Audio Dynamics
operators.translation: This controls how loud sounds get over time, like squashing loud parts and lifting quiet ones.

operators.name: Audio File In
operators.translation: This plays sound from a file over time.

operators.name: Audio Filter
operators.translation: This removes or changes certain sound frequencies.

operators.name: Audio Movie
operators.translation: This pulls sound out of a movie file and plays it.

operators.name: Audio NDI
operators.translation: This receives live audio over a network.

operators.name: Audio Oscillator
operators.translation: This makes a repeating sound wave, like a steady beep.

operators.name: Audio Power EQ
operators.translation: This shapes sound by boosting or cutting energy in different frequency bands.

operators.name: Audio Play
operators.translation: This controls when sound starts, stops, and pauses.

operators.name: Audio Render
operators.translation: This mixes sound channels together so they can be heard.

operators.name: Audio Spectrum
operators.translation: This shows how strong different sound frequencies are over time.

operators.name: Audio Stream In
operators.translation: This receives live audio from another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Audio Stream Out
operators.translation: This sends live audio to another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Audio VST
operators.translation: This runs a plugin that changes or generates sound.

operators.name: Audio Web Render
operators.translation: This pulls sound from a web page and plays it over time.

operators.name: Bass
operators.translation: This focuses on low sounds, like the thump you feel in music.

operators.name: Beat
operators.translation: This listens for the pulse in music and keeps track of it.

operators.name: Bind
operators.translation: This connects one value to another so they move together.

operators.name: Blacktrax
operators.translation: This brings in live position data from a BlackTrax tracking system.

operators.name: Blob Track
operators.translation: This follows moving shapes over time and remembers where they were.

operators.name: Body Track
operators.translation: This tracks body joints and movement from a sensor.

operators.name: Bullet Solver
operators.translation: This steps physics forward in time and remembers past motion.

operators.name: Clip
operators.translation: This limits values so they stay inside a set range.

operators.name: Clip Blender
operators.translation: This blends between different clips of motion over time.

operators.name: Clock
operators.translation: This generates time signals like seconds, minutes, or beats.

operators.name: Composite
operators.translation: This combines channels using rules like add or multiply.

operators.name: Constant
operators.translation: This outputs a steady value that stays the same.

operators.name: Copy
operators.translation: This duplicates channels and lines them up in new ways.

operators.name: Count
operators.translation: This counts steps or events as time moves forward.

operators.name: DAT to CHOP
operators.translation: This turns table data into time-based channels.

operators.name: Delay
operators.translation: This shifts values back in time so they arrive later.

operators.name: DMX In
operators.translation: This receives live lighting control values from a DMX system.

operators.name: DMX Out
operators.translation: This sends lighting control values to a DMX system.

operators.name: Envelope
operators.translation: This shapes how a value changes over time, like how a sound slowly fades in and out.

operators.name: Event
operators.translation: This watches for something to happen and reacts when it does.

operators.name: Expression
operators.translation: This calculates values using math formulas whenever it updates.

operators.name: Extend
operators.translation: This decides what happens to values outside their original time range.

operators.name: Face Track
operators.translation: This tracks facial features over time and remembers their positions.

operators.name: Fan
operators.translation: This spreads one channel into many, like dealing cards from a deck.

operators.name: Feedback
operators.translation: This feeds previous values back into the next step, creating history.

operators.name: File In
operators.translation: This loads channel data from a file.

operators.name: File Out
operators.translation: This saves channel data to a file.

operators.name: Filter
operators.translation: This smooths or changes values over time.

operators.name: Function
operators.translation: This creates channels by following a math rule.

operators.name: Gesture
operators.translation: This looks for patterns in movement over time.

operators.name: Handle
operators.translation: This gives you something to grab in the viewer to move or adjust values.

operators.name: Hog
operators.translation: This brings in live lighting control data from a Hog console.

operators.name: Hokuyo
operators.translation: This reads distance measurements from a laser scanner as changing numbers.

operators.name: Hold
operators.translation: This keeps the last value and holds onto it until something new arrives.

operators.name: Import Select
operators.translation: This pulls channels from somewhere else in the project.

operators.name: In
operators.translation: This receives channels coming into a component.

operators.name: Info
operators.translation: This reports information about another operator as numbers.

operators.name: Interpolate
operators.translation: This fills in smooth steps between values over time.

operators.name: Immersive Curve
operators.translation: This outputs curve data used for immersive display setups.

operators.name: Inverse Kin
operators.translation: This figures out how joints should bend so an end point reaches a target.

operators.name: Join
operators.translation: This sticks channels together end-to-end in time.

operators.name: Joystick
operators.translation: This reads live input from a joystick or game controller.

operators.name: Keyboard In
operators.translation: This turns key presses into changing values.

operators.name: Keyframe
operators.translation: This lets you draw values over time using key points.

operators.name: Kinect / Connect
operators.translation: This connects to a Kinect and brings in body or depth data.

operators.name: Lag
operators.translation: This makes values react slowly instead of changing instantly.

operators.name: Laser
operators.translation: This sends control data to a laser output system.

operators.name: Leap Motion
operators.translation: This brings in live hand and finger movement data.

operators.name: Leuze ROD4
operators.translation: This reads distance and position data from an industrial sensor.

operators.name: LFO
operators.translation: This makes repeating waves, like slow oscillating motion.

operators.name: Limit
operators.translation: This restricts how fast or how far values are allowed to change.

operators.name: Logic
operators.translation: This makes yes-or-no decisions using rules, like checking if a switch is on or off.

operators.name: Lookup
operators.translation: This uses one set of values to look up results in another set, like using a chart to find answers.

operators.name: LTC In
operators.translation: This reads timecode from an audio signal so everything can stay in sync.

operators.name: LTC Out
operators.translation: This turns time information into an audio signal for syncing other devices.

operators.name: MIDI In
operators.translation: This receives live note and control data from a MIDI device.

operators.name: MIDI In Map
operators.translation: This connects incoming MIDI controls to specific channels.

operators.name: MIDI Out
operators.translation: This sends MIDI notes or controls to external devices.

operators.name: MOSIS
operators.translation: This brings in motion data from a MOSIS tracking system.

operators.name: Mouse In
operators.translation: This turns mouse movement and clicks into changing values.

operators.name: Mouse Out
operators.translation: This sends values out as mouse control signals.

operators.name: NCam
operators.translation: This brings in live camera tracking data as channels.

operators.name: Noise
operators.translation: This generates random-looking values over time.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This passes values through unchanged and gives them a clear name.

operators.name: Oak Device
operators.translation: This connects to an Oak depth camera and brings in live sensor data.

operators.name: Oak Select
operators.translation: This chooses specific channels coming from an Oak device.

operators.name: Object
operators.translation: This pulls transform or state values from a 3D object into channels.

operators.name: OpenVR
operators.translation: This brings in headset and controller movement from a VR system.

operators.name: OptiTrack In
operators.translation: This receives live motion-capture data from an OptiTrack system.

operators.name: OCS In
operators.translation: This receives control data from an OCS network source.

operators.name: OCS Out
operators.translation: This sends control data out to an OCS network destination.

operators.name: Out
operators.translation: This marks channels as outputs of a component.

operators.name: Pan Tilt
operators.translation: This converts control values into pan and tilt motion for devices.

operators.name: Panel
operators.translation: This reads values from interface panels like sliders and buttons.

operators.name: Pangolin
operators.translation: This sends control data to Pangolin laser systems.

operators.name: Parameter
operators.translation: This turns parameter values into channels that update when the parameter changes.

operators.name: Pattern
operators.translation: This creates repeating value patterns over time.

operators.name: Perform
operators.translation: This manages timing and state during live performance playback.

operators.name: Phaser
operators.translation: This shifts phases in sound or signals, creating a sweeping effect.

operators.name: Pipe In
operators.translation: This receives channel data from another process through a system pipe.

operators.name: Pop
operators.translation: This makes a short spike when a value changes, like a quick tap instead of a long press.

operators.name: Pop 2
operators.translation: This is another way to make short spikes, with more control over how they fire.

operators.name: PosiStageNet
operators.translation: This brings in live position data used for stage lighting and tracking.

operators.name: Pulse
operators.translation: This creates a momentary on signal, like pressing a button very fast.

operators.name: Record
operators.translation: This records channel values over time so they can be played back later.

operators.name: Rename
operators.translation: This changes channel names without changing the data.

operators.name: Render Pick
operators.translation: This reads which 3D object the mouse is pointing at in a rendered scene.

operators.name: Render Stream In
operators.translation: This receives channel data streamed from another TouchDesigner process.

operators.name: Reorder
operators.translation: This rearranges channels into a new order.

operators.name: Replace
operators.translation: This swaps channel values using another channel as a guide.

operators.name: Resample
operators.translation: This changes how often values are sampled over time.

operators.name: S-Curve
operators.translation: This reshapes values using a smooth S-shaped curve.

operators.name: Script
operators.translation: This runs code that can change channel data whenever it updates.

operators.name: Select
operators.translation: This chooses specific channels and ignores the rest.

operators.name: Sequencer
operators.translation: This plays values in steps over time, like following a timeline.

operators.name: Serial
operators.translation: This reads data coming in over a serial port from external hardware.

operators.name: Shared Memory In
operators.translation: This reads channel data from a shared memory space.

operators.name: Shared Memory Out
operators.translation: This writes channel data into shared memory for other programs to read.

operators.name: Shift
operators.translation: This moves channel values forward or backward in time, like sliding them left or right.

operators.name: Shuffle
operators.translation: This rearranges channels or samples into a new layout.

operators.name: Slope
operators.translation: This turns changes over time into speed-like values.

operators.name: SOP to CHOP
operators.translation: This turns 3D shape data into numeric channels.

operators.name: Sort
operators.translation: This orders channel values from smallest to biggest or the other way around.

operators.name: Speed
operators.translation: This measures how fast values are changing.

operators.name: Splice
operators.translation: This smoothly joins different pieces of channel data together.

operators.name: Spring
operators.translation: This makes values move like they are attached to a spring, with bounce and drag.

operators.name: ST 2110 Device
operators.translation: This connects to a professional broadcast video device and reads its timing data.

operators.name: Stretch
operators.translation: This squeezes or expands channel data in time.

operators.name: SType In
operators.translation: This receives data using the SType protocol from another system.

operators.name: SType Out
operators.translation: This sends data out using the SType protocol.

operators.name: Switch
operators.translation: This chooses which channels pass through, like flipping between inputs.

operators.name: Sync In
operators.translation: This receives timing signals so different systems stay in step.

operators.name: Sync Out
operators.translation: This sends timing signals to keep other systems in step.

operators.name: Tablet
operators.translation: This reads pen and tablet input like position and pressure.

operators.name: Time Slice
operators.translation: This processes channels in small chunks of time instead of all at once.

operators.name: Timer
operators.translation: This counts time for things like delays, loops, or countdowns.

operators.name: TOP to CHOP
operators.translation: This turns image pixels into numeric channels.

operators.name: Touch In
operators.translation: This receives channel data from another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Touch Out
operators.translation: This sends channel data to another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Trail
operators.translation: This keeps past values around to draw or analyze motion paths.

operators.name: Transform
operators.translation: This changes channel values by scaling, rotating, or offsetting them.

operators.name: Transform XYZ
operators.translation: This applies position, rotation, and scale changes using X, Y, and Z values.

operators.name: Trigger
operators.translation: This watches for a signal crossing a line and then fires an action.

operators.name: Trim
operators.translation: This cuts channel data so only a chosen time range remains.

operators.name: Warp
operators.translation: This bends or remaps channel values in time.

operators.name: Wave
operators.translation: This makes repeating wave shapes like sine or square patterns.

operators.name: ZED
operators.translation: This brings in stereo camera and depth data from a ZED device.

operators.name: Add
operators.translation: This connects points together to make lines or shapes, depending on the order you choose.

operators.name: Alembic
operators.translation: This brings in animated 3D shapes from a file made somewhere else.

operators.name: Align
operators.translation: This lines shapes up so they match position or direction.

operators.name: Arm
operators.translation: This builds a chain of joints for moving a character or object.

operators.name: Attribute
operators.translation: This reads information stored on points or shapes and exposes it.

operators.name: Attribute Create
operators.translation: This adds new information to points or shapes, like labels or extra numbers.

operators.name: Basis
operators.translation: This changes how curves are calculated, affecting how smooth they are.

operators.name: Blend
operators.translation: This mixes two shapes together so one slowly turns into the other.

operators.name: Bone Group
operators.translation: This organizes bones into named groups so they can be controlled together.

operators.name: Boolean
operators.translation: This cuts or combines shapes using overlap rules, like subtracting one cookie cutter from another.

operators.name: Box
operators.translation: This creates a simple box shape.

operators.name: Bridge
operators.translation: This connects edges or shapes together to span a gap.

operators.name: Cache
operators.translation: This stores geometry from earlier frames so it can be reused.

operators.name: Cap
operators.translation: This fills holes in shapes so they become closed.

operators.name: Capture
operators.translation: This binds points to bones so they move together during animation.

operators.name: Capture Region
operators.translation: This shows or defines which bones affect which parts of a shape.

operators.name: Carve
operators.translation: This cuts a curve so only part of it remains.

operators.name: CHOP To
operators.translation: This uses time-based numbers to move or change shapes.

operators.name: Circle
operators.translation: This creates a round curve or flat circle shape.

operators.name: Clay
operators.translation: This smooths and reshapes geometry like pushing real clay around.

operators.name: Clip
operators.translation: This cuts geometry using a plane, like slicing a model with a knife.

operators.name: Convert
operators.translation: This changes geometry from one type into another, like turning curves into polygons.

operators.name: Copy
operators.translation: This makes many copies of a shape and places them using rules.

operators.name: Creep
operators.translation: This slides points along a surface, like ants crawling over an object.

operators.name: Curve Clay
operators.translation: This lets you push and smooth curves as if they were soft wire.

operators.name: Curve Set (Check / Intersect Curves)
operators.translation: This checks where curves cross or touch each other.

operators.name: DAT to SOP
operators.translation: This turns table data into 3D shapes.

operators.name: Deform
operators.translation: This bends or reshapes geometry using another object or field.

operators.name: Delete
operators.translation: This removes parts of geometry based on rules.

operators.name: Divide
operators.translation: This splits shapes into smaller pieces.

operators.name: Extrude
operators.translation: This pulls a flat shape outward to give it depth.

operators.name: Face Track
operators.translation: This turns live face tracking data into moving geometry.

operators.name: Facet
operators.translation: This changes how surfaces are smoothed or split at edges.

operators.name: File In
operators.translation: This loads geometry from a file.

operators.name: Fillet
operators.translation: This rounds off sharp edges.

operators.name: Fit
operators.translation: This rescales geometry so it fits within a space.

operators.name: Force
operators.translation: This pushes geometry using simulated forces.

operators.name: Fractal
operators.translation: This adds repeating rough detail to shapes.

operators.name: Grid
operators.translation: This creates a flat grid of points or polygons.

operators.name: Group
operators.translation: This puts parts of geometry into named groups so you can work on them together.

operators.name: Edit
operators.translation: This lets you grab and move points or shapes by hand.

operators.name: Hole
operators.translation: This cuts holes in surfaces using shapes or rules.

operators.name: Import Select
operators.translation: This pulls geometry from another component or network.

operators.name: In
operators.translation: This brings geometry into a component from the outside.

operators.name: Inverse Curve
operators.translation: This reverses or flips how a curve is measured.

operators.name: Isosurface
operators.translation: This turns a volume or field into a visible surface.

operators.name: Joint
operators.translation: This creates a joint used for skeletal movement.

operators.name: Kinect / Connect
operators.translation: This brings live body or depth data from a Kinect into geometry.

operators.name: Lattice
operators.translation: This bends geometry using a cage, like squeezing something inside a box.

operators.name: Limit
operators.translation: This restricts how far points are allowed to move.

operators.name: Line
operators.translation: This creates a straight line shape.

operators.name: Line Thick
operators.translation: This turns a thin line into a thick ribbon or tube.

operators.name: LOD
operators.translation: This switches between simpler or more detailed shapes depending on conditions.

operators.name: LS System
operators.translation: This grows branching structures like plants using rules.

operators.name: Magnet
operators.translation: This pulls points toward a target like a magnet.

operators.name: Material
operators.translation: This assigns surface appearance information to geometry.

operators.name: Merge
operators.translation: This combines multiple pieces of geometry into one.

operators.name: Metaball
operators.translation: This creates blobby shapes that melt into each other.

operators.name: Model
operators.translation: This applies transformations like move, rotate, or scale.

operators.name: Noise
operators.translation: This adds random variation to geometry.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This passes geometry through unchanged and gives it a clear name.

operators.name: Object Merge
operators.translation: This pulls geometry from another object or network into the current one.

operators.name: OpenVR
operators.translation: This brings headset or controller position data into geometry space.

operators.name: Out
operators.translation: This marks geometry as the output of a component.

operators.name: Particle
operators.translation: This moves points over time like tiny floating objects.

operators.name: Point
operators.translation: This changes or reads information stored on individual points.

operators.name: PolyLoft
operators.translation: This stretches surfaces between curves to form a shape.

operators.name: Point Groups
operators.translation: This organizes points into named sets.

operators.name: PolyPatch
operators.translation: This fills areas with smooth polygon surfaces.

operators.name: PolyReduce
operators.translation: This removes extra polygons to make geometry simpler.

operators.name: PolySpline
operators.translation: This smooths polygon edges into curved lines.

operators.name: PolyStitch
operators.translation: This sews separate polygon edges together.

operators.name: POP2
operators.translation: This simulates particles using forces and rules over time.

operators.name: Primitive
operators.translation: This works on whole faces or shapes instead of individual points.

operators.name: Face Hull
operators.translation: This wraps geometry in the smallest possible outer surface.

operators.name: Meta
operators.translation: This combines influence fields to create soft blobby surfaces.

operators.name: Particles
operators.translation: This generates and updates many moving points over time.

operators.name: Profile
operators.translation: This defines a cross-section shape used by other tools.

operators.name: Project
operators.translation: This projects geometry onto another surface.

operators.name: Rails
operators.translation: This builds surfaces using guide curves like train tracks.

operators.name: Raster
operators.translation: This turns shapes into a grid-based surface.

operators.name: Ray
operators.translation: This shoots invisible lines from points until they hit another surface.

operators.name: Rectangle
operators.translation: This creates a flat rectangular shape.

operators.name: Refine
operators.translation: This adds more points to curves or surfaces to make them smoother.

operators.name: Resample
operators.translation: This redistributes points evenly along curves.

operators.name: Resolve
operators.translation: This fixes overlapping or conflicting geometry relationships.

operators.name: Script
operators.translation: This uses code to change geometry when it updates.

operators.name: Select
operators.translation: This chooses specific parts of geometry and ignores the rest.

operators.name: Sequence Blend
operators.translation: This blends between multiple shapes in a specific order.

operators.name: Skin
operators.translation: This stretches a surface over bones so it can move with them.

operators.name: Sort
operators.translation: This rearranges points or shapes based on rules.

operators.name: Sphere
operators.translation: This creates a round 3D ball shape.

operators.name: Spring
operators.translation: This makes points move like they are attached to springs.

operators.name: Sprinkle
operators.translation: This scatters points across a surface.

operators.name: Sprite
operators.translation: This turns points into camera-facing images.

operators.name: Stitch
operators.translation: This sews edges of geometry together.

operators.name: Subdivide
operators.translation: This splits surfaces into smaller pieces to make them smoother.

operators.name: Superquad / Supersquad
operators.translation: This creates flexible shapes that can morph between cubes and spheres.

operators.name: Surfsect
operators.translation: This finds where surfaces intersect each other.

operators.name: Surfsect
operators.translation: This finds the lines where two surfaces cut through each other.

operators.name: Sweep
operators.translation: This pulls a shape along a path to make a surface, like dragging a ribbon through space.

operators.name: Switch
operators.translation: This chooses which geometry passes through, like selecting one track at a time.

operators.name: Text
operators.translation: This turns letters into shapes you can use as geometry.

operators.name: Texture
operators.translation: This assigns image-based information to geometry for later use.

operators.name: Torus
operators.translation: This creates a donut-shaped 3D object.

operators.name: Trace
operators.translation: This turns image edges into line geometry.

operators.name: Trail
operators.translation: This keeps past positions to draw motion paths.

operators.name: Transform
operators.translation: This moves, rotates, or scales geometry.

operators.name: Trim
operators.translation: This keeps only part of a curve or surface.

operators.name: TriStrip
operators.translation: This turns polygons into triangle strips for efficient drawing.

operators.name: Tube
operators.translation: This creates a hollow cylinder shape.

operators.name: Twist
operators.translation: This spins geometry around an axis.

operators.name: Vertex
operators.translation: This works on individual corner points of polygons.

operators.name: Wireframe
operators.translation: This turns surfaces into line outlines.

operators.name: ZED (SOP)
operators.translation: This turns live ZED camera depth and tracking data into geometry.

operators.name: Accumulate
operators.translation: This keeps adding changes over time, like counting steps as you walk.

operators.name: Activity Division
operators.translation: This splits particles into groups based on what they are doing.

operators.name: Alembic In
operators.translation: This brings animated particle or geometry data in from an Alembic file.

operators.name: Analyze
operators.translation: This measures things about particles, like speed or distance.

operators.name: Attribute
operators.translation: This reads information stored on particles, like color or age.

operators.name: Attribute Convert
operators.translation: This changes particle data from one type into another.

operators.name: Blend
operators.translation: This mixes particle states together, like fading from one pose to another.

operators.name: Box
operators.translation: This creates a box-shaped region used by particles.

operators.name: Cache
operators.translation: This stores particle states from earlier frames.

operators.name: Cache Blend
operators.translation: This blends between stored particle states over time.

operators.name: Cache Select
operators.translation: This chooses a stored particle frame from memory.

operators.name: CHOP to
operators.translation: This uses time-based channel data to control particles.

operators.name: Circle
operators.translation: This creates a circular region or shape for particles.

operators.name: Connectivity
operators.translation: This figures out which particles are connected or near each other.

operators.name: Convert
operators.translation: This changes particle data into another form or structure.

operators.name: Copy
operators.translation: This duplicates particles using rules.

operators.name: C++
operators.translation: This runs custom code to control how particles behave.

operators.name: Curve
operators.translation: This creates or uses a curve to guide particle motion.

operators.name: DAT to
operators.translation: This turns table data into particle information.

operators.name: Dimension
operators.translation: This changes how many dimensions particle data uses.

operators.name: DMX Fixture
operators.translation: This represents a real lighting fixture so particles can control its channels.

operators.name: DMX Out
operators.translation: This sends particle-driven values out to a DMX lighting system.

operators.name: DMX Profile
operators.translation: This describes how a lighting fixture understands its control values.

operators.name: Extrude
operators.translation: This pulls particle shapes outward to give them depth.

operators.name: Facet
operators.translation: This changes how particle surfaces are smoothed or split.

operators.name: Feedback
operators.translation: This feeds previous particle states back into the next frame.

operators.name: Field
operators.translation: This creates an invisible influence that pushes particles around.

operators.name: File In
operators.translation: This loads particle data from a file.

operators.name: File Out
operators.translation: This saves particle data to a file.

operators.name: Force
operators.translation: This applies pushes or pulls to particles over time.

operators.name: Global
operators.translation: This defines global rules that affect all particles.

operators.name: GLSL Advanced
operators.translation: This uses custom GPU programs to control particle behavior.

operators.name: GLSL Compute Shader
operators.translation: This updates particles using compute programs on the graphics card.

operators.name: GLSL Copy
operators.translation: This copies particle data using GPU code.

operators.name: GLSL Select
operators.translation: This selects which particle attributes a shader works on.

operators.name: Grid
operators.translation: This creates a grid used by particles as a space or surface.

operators.name: Group
operators.translation: This puts particles into named groups.

operators.name: Histogram
operators.translation: This measures how particle values are distributed.

operators.name: Import Select
operators.translation: This pulls particle data from another network or component.

operators.name: In
operators.translation: This receives particles from outside the current component.

operators.name: Limit
operators.translation: This keeps particle values from going past set limits.

operators.name: Line
operators.translation: This creates a straight line used by particles as a guide or shape.

operators.name: Line Divide
operators.translation: This splits a line into evenly spaced pieces.

operators.name: Line Resample
operators.translation: This redistributes points evenly along a line.

operators.name: Line Smooth
operators.translation: This smooths a line so sharp changes become gentle curves.

operators.name: Lookup Attribute
operators.translation: This looks up particle values using other particle attributes.

operators.name: Lookup Channel
operators.translation: This uses channel data to look up particle values.

operators.name: Lookup Texture
operators.translation: This uses an image to decide particle values.

operators.name: Math
operators.translation: This applies math rules to particle values.

operators.name: Math Combine
operators.translation: This combines multiple particle streams using math rules.

operators.name: Merge
operators.translation: This combines multiple particle systems into one.

operators.name: Metrics
operators.translation: This measures distances and relationships between particles.

operators.name: Neighbor
operators.translation: This finds nearby particles and relates them to each other.

operators.name: Noise
operators.translation: This adds random motion or variation to particles.

operators.name: Normalize
operators.translation: This rescales particle values so they fit into a standard range.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This passes particles through unchanged and gives them a clear name.

operators.name: OAK Select
operators.translation: This selects specific data coming from an OAK device.

operators.name: Out
operators.translation: This marks particles as the output of a component.

operators.name: Particle
operators.translation: This creates and updates moving particles over time.

operators.name: Pattern
operators.translation: This generates repeating value patterns for particles.

operators.name: Phaser
operators.translation: This shifts phases in particle values, creating sweeping motion.

operators.name: Plane
operators.translation: This creates a flat plane used as a surface or boundary for particles.

operators.name: Point File In
operators.translation: This loads point data from a file into particles.

operators.name: Point Generator
operators.translation: This creates points that particles can use as positions.

operators.name: Points
operators.translation: This works directly with particle point data.

operators.name: Polygonize
operators.translation: This turns soft or point-based data into flat polygon shapes.

operators.name: Primitive
operators.translation: This works on whole particle shapes instead of individual points.

operators.name: Projection
operators.translation: This projects particle data onto another surface or space.

operators.name: Proximity
operators.translation: This checks how close particles are to each other.

operators.name: Quantize
operators.translation: This snaps values into steps instead of letting them change smoothly.

operators.name: Random
operators.translation: This creates unpredictable values for particles.

operators.name: Ray
operators.translation: This shoots rays from particles until they hit something.

operators.name: Rectangle
operators.translation: This creates a rectangular region used by particles.

operators.name: Rearrange
operators.translation: This reorganizes particle data into a new layout.

operators.name: Rerange
operators.translation: This remaps values from one range into another.

operators.name: Select
operators.translation: This chooses which particle data to keep.

operators.name: Serial
operators.translation: This receives particle-related data from hardware over a serial connection.

operators.name: Skin
operators.translation: This stretches surfaces over particles so they move together.

operators.name: Skin Deform
operators.translation: This deforms shapes based on particle-driven skinning.

operators.name: SOP to
operators.translation: This uses geometry shapes to control particles.

operators.name: Sort
operators.translation: This rearranges particles based on rules like distance or age.

operators.name: Sphere
operators.translation: This creates a spherical region for particles.

operators.name: Sprinkle
operators.translation: This scatters particles across a surface.

operators.name: Subdivide
operators.translation: This splits particle geometry into smaller pieces.

operators.name: Switch
operators.translation: This chooses which particle stream is active.

operators.name: Texture Map
operators.translation: This uses an image to control particle values.

operators.name: TOP to
operators.translation: This uses image data to drive particles.

operators.name: Topology
operators.translation: This describes how particles are connected.

operators.name: Torus
operators.translation: This creates a donut-shaped region for particles.

operators.name: Trail
operators.translation: This keeps a history of particle motion.

operators.name: Transform
operators.translation: This moves, rotates, or scales particles.

operators.name: Trig
operators.translation: This uses sine and cosine to shape particle motion.

operators.name: Tube
operators.translation: This creates a tube-shaped region for particles.

operators.name: Twist
operators.translation: This spins particle positions around an axis.

operators.name: ZED
operators.translation: This brings live depth and tracking data from a ZED camera into particles.

operators.name: Artnet
operators.translation: This sends or receives lighting control messages over a network.

operators.name: CHOP Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when channel values change.

operators.name: CHOP to
operators.translation: This turns time-based numbers into rows of text data.

operators.name: CSN
operators.translation: This communicates with external systems using the CSN protocol.

operators.name: C++
operators.translation: This runs custom code that can create or change table data.

operators.name: DAT Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when table data changes.

operators.name: Error
operators.translation: This shows error messages as readable text.

operators.name: Etherdream
operators.translation: This sends laser control data to Ether Dream hardware.

operators.name: Evaluate
operators.translation: This calculates expressions and shows the results as text.

operators.name: Examine
operators.translation: This lets you inspect data in a readable way.

operators.name: FIFO
operators.translation: This stores rows of data in a line where the oldest comes out first.

operators.name: File In
operators.translation: This loads text or table data from a file.

operators.name: File Out
operators.translation: This saves table data to a file.

operators.name: Folder
operators.translation: This lists files and folders as table rows.

operators.name: Info
operators.translation: This reports information about another operator as text.

operators.name: Insert
operators.translation: This adds new rows or columns into a table.

operators.name: JSON
operators.translation: This reads or writes data using the JSON format.

operators.name: Keyboard In
operators.translation: This turns key presses into table entries.

operators.name: Lookup
operators.translation: This finds table values using other values as keys.

operators.name: Media File In
operators.translation: This reads metadata or information from media files.

operators.name: Merge
operators.translation: This combines multiple tables into one.

operators.name: MIDI Event
operators.translation: This shows MIDI messages as rows of text.

operators.name: MIDI In
operators.translation: This receives MIDI data and lists it in a table.

operators.name: Monitors
operators.translation: This lists connected displays and their properties.

operators.name: MPCDI
operators.translation: This loads projection calibration data into tables.

operators.name: MQTT Client
operators.translation: This sends and receives messages using MQTT.

operators.name: Multi-Touch In
operators.translation: This turns touch input into table rows.

operators.name: NDI
operators.translation: This lists or controls network video sources.

operators.name: Null
operators.translation: This passes table data through unchanged and gives it a name.

operators.name: OP Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when operators change state.

operators.name: OP Find
operators.translation: This searches for operators by name or type and lists the matches.

operators.name: OSC In
operators.translation: This receives OSC messages over a network and turns them into table rows.

operators.name: Out
operators.translation: This marks a table as the output of a component.

operators.name: Panel Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when UI panels change, like button presses or slider moves.

operators.name: Parameter Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when a parameter changes.

operators.name: Par Group Execute
operators.translation: This runs actions when a group of parameters changes.

operators.name: Perform
operators.translation: This manages perform mode info and control as table data.

operators.name: POP to
operators.translation: This turns particle data into table rows.

operators.name: Reorder
operators.translation: This rearranges rows or columns into a new order.

operators.name: Render Pick
operators.translation: This reports what object is being pointed at in a rendered view.

operators.name: Script
operators.translation: This runs code that can create or modify table data.

operators.name: Select
operators.translation: This chooses specific rows, columns, or cells and ignores the rest.

operators.name: Serial
operators.translation: This reads messages from hardware over a serial port into table rows.

operators.name: Serial Device
operators.translation: This lists and helps configure serial ports on the computer.

operators.name: Socket IO
operators.translation: This connects to a Socket.IO server and sends/receives messages as table data.

operators.name: SOP to
operators.translation: This turns 3D geometry data into table rows.

operators.name: Sort
operators.translation: This orders rows based on values, like sorting a list.

operators.name: Substitute
operators.translation: This finds text and replaces it with other text.

operators.name: Switch
operators.translation: This chooses which table to output from multiple inputs.

operators.name: Table
operators.translation: This stores a table of cells you can edit and keep.

operators.name: TCP/IP
operators.translation: This sends and receives messages over TCP, like a reliable phone call connection.

operators.name: Text
operators.translation: This stores and edits text, like a notepad inside the project.

operators.name: Touch In
operators.translation: This receives table data from another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Touch Out
operators.translation: This sends table data to another TouchDesigner project.

operators.name: Transpose
operators.translation: This swaps rows and columns, like turning a table sideways.

operators.name: TUIO In
operators.translation: This receives TUIO touch-tracking messages and turns them into table rows.

operators.name: UDP In
operators.translation: This receives UDP messages and lists them as table data.

operators.name: Video Devices
operators.translation: This lists available video capture devices and their settings.

operators.name: Web Client
operators.translation: This fetches web pages or data from the internet into table rows.

operators.name: Web Server
operators.translation: This hosts a web service so other devices can request data from your project.

operators.name: WebRTC
operators.translation: This sends and receives live audio/video/data using WebRTC connections.

operators.name: WebSocket
operators.translation: This keeps a live two-way web connection open and streams messages.

operators.name: XML
operators.translation: This reads or writes data in XML format.
